question: Map the Ascend SASG ScatterAdd implementation to the AMD sparse-MLA compressed dKV many-to-one wall, and fold the MoE-style routing analogy into the next kernel contract.

facts: SparseAttnSharedkvGrad is documented as a five-stage backward operator.
The stages are Gather, Cube12, Process, Cube345, and Scatter.
The recipe explicitly says the pipeline preloads Gather+Cube12 once to achieve CV pipeline parallelism.
AIC runs CubeCompute over t1/n2/selected loops.
AIV runs VecCompute over the same task stream.
CubeCompute preloads cube12, signals vector work, waits on the prior vector stage, then runs cube345 for the previous ping-pong slot.
VecCompute waits for Cube12 output, runs vector Process, signals Cube345, and flips ping-pong state.
Cube345 runs cube5, then cube4, then cube3.
cube4/cube5 results are written to mm4ResWorkspaceGm and mm5ResWorkspaceGm.
The workspace allocation reserves the ScatterAdd staging slab before binding mm4ResWorkspaceGm/mm5ResWorkspaceGm.
cube4 computes the ds^T @ query style contribution for dK and writes currentOutGmOffset into mm4ResWorkspaceGm.
cube5 computes the p^T @ dO style contribution for dV and writes currentOutGmOffset into mm5ResWorkspaceGm.
It reads dk rows from mm4ResWorkspaceGm and dv rows from mm5ResWorkspaceGm into UB buffers in 16-row chunks.

It scales dk by scaleValue, adds the value-side contribution, resolves compressed versus original KV destination through topk indices/original window metadata, and DataCopy writes into dCmpKvOutGm or dOriKvOutGm under atomic-add mode.

fast_sparse_atomic_gate.read: The fast query-major Triton producer performs tl.dot contribution math and immediately atomic-adds each selected KxD contribution to indirect compressed dKV.

compact_partial_near_miss.read: Compact partial-store keeps the fast query-major producer and emits reduced per-seq-tile active-group rows, but the detached global partial write plus final index_add loses the latency race.

pair_slot_reducer_rejected.read: The pair-slot reducer validates reduced global writes but regresses because it performs one local-table update per selected contribution before emit.

group_outer_loop_rejected_for_production.read: Prepacked group segments are good writeback owners but poor contribution-production owners because each group segment has at most one selected row per token in the measured S4096 layer-2 route.

transferable: Treat (token, selected_slot) -> compressed_group as a routed ownership problem, analogous to (token, selected_slot) -> expert in MoE.
Prepack routes so the destination owner sees compact rows instead of random global atomics.
Reduce locally before the final global write.
not_transferable: Do not make compressed group the outermost contribution-production loop in the same way expert-major MoE kernels make expert the GEMM owner.
MoE top-k is small and expert compute is a large MLP GEMM; LI layer-2 topk is 512, so group-owned production reloads/recomputes q and dO across many groups and loses the producer reuse.
moe_routing_lesson.resulting_contract: Use MoE-style route compaction for reduction/writeback ownership, but keep dense-attention-style query-major MFMA/tl.dot contribution production.

ascend_to_amd_mapping.gather: Route/topk metadata and selected compressed/original KV fetch. AMD already has cheap actual-LI route packing and active-group metadata, but selected-state materialization is not acceptable for production.
ascend_to_amd_mapping.cube12: QK and dO/V dot work before softmax/backward row terms. AMD forward/shared-KV schedules are viable; backward must keep the fast query-major dot loop.
ascend_to_amd_mapping.process: Softmax and row-gradient terms. AMD target-prob and KL derivative probes are already sub-ms; this is not the current wall.
ascend_to_amd_mapping.cube345: dQ plus dK/dV contribution math. AMD fast dot-K producer maps here and is already close when measured without final many-to-one global accumulation.
ascend_to_amd_mapping.scatter: Final scaled dk + dv add and atomic writeback. Ascend still has atomic ScatterAdd, but it is fused into the operator pipeline and fed by Cube4/Cube5 workspace rows. AMD detached raw ScatterAdd is negative because it adds HBM partial traffic and no useful overlap.

next_kernel_rung.name: query_major_segmented_scatteradd_v1
next_kernel_rung.goal: Keep the current dot_k_atomic query-major tl.dot producer, but insert sparse keyed segmented accumulation before global dKV writes so ScatterAdd sees fewer already-reduced rows.
mechanism: Within the token-major selected-tile loop, compute contrib_kd with the existing tl.dot schedule.
Use prepacked route metadata to form short sorted or slot-tagged runs for the selected tile.
Accumulate duplicate compressed groups at lane/warp/block scope before either touching global dKV or a compact partial workspace.
Emit one/few rows per (seq_tile, selected_tile, compressed_group, d16) instead of one row per (token, selected_slot, d16).
Use the already-cheap compact reducer only after the producer has reduced the stream enough to beat the direct atomic wall.
acceptance_gate: S128 parity against the existing dot_k_atomic output within established BF16 tolerance.
S512 timing does not regress relative to dot_k_atomic.
S4096 layer-2 median beats the 8.0069 ms same-tuple sparse-atomic gate including route-pack and final reduce cost.
Promotion target for the sparse-backward ladder is less than 6.5 ms for compressed dKV, with stretch target 3-4 ms to re-approach the old sparse-MLA backward envelope.
explicit_non_goals: No group-outer contribution-production kernel as the next rung.
No dense active_group x selected_K route matrix.
No pair-slot LDS/local-table update per selected contribution.
No detached raw ScatterAdd HBM round trip as a promoted latency path.

decision: Ascend source confirms ScatterAdd belongs to the final SASG Scatter stage and still uses atomic add; the optimization is pipeline placement and reduced/structured inputs, not removing the scatter semantics. For AMD, the next speedup candidate should be a query-major segmented ScatterAdd producer: MoE-like routed ownership for writeback, dense-attention-like query-major MFMA reuse for contribution math.
